title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is less about new frontier model capabilities than about the infrastructure around deployment: guidance for safer use, incident response, child protections, and a few research papers probing privacy, evaluation, and incentive risks. The mix suggests continued movement toward operational governance and measurement, even if the freshest academic items are early-stage and uneven in direct policy relevance.

items.title: OpenAI publishes responsible and safe use guidance
items.why_it_matters: It codifies practical safety, accuracy, and transparency norms for real-world AI deployment.

items.title: OpenAI details response to Axios developer tool compromise
items.why_it_matters: Supply-chain incident handling is central to securing AI software distribution and protecting downstream users.

items.title: OpenAI introduces Child Safety Blueprint
items.why_it_matters: It frames age-appropriate safeguards and governance expectations for AI systems used by minors.

items.title: Differentially Private Language Generation and Identification in the Limit
items.why_it_matters: It advances theory for privacy-preserving language generation under continual release settings.

items.title: Ads in AI Chatbots? An Analysis of How Large Language Models Navigate Conflicts of Interest
items.why_it_matters: It examines whether monetization incentives could distort model behavior against user interests.

items.title: ClawBench: Can AI Agents Complete Everyday Online Tasks?
items.why_it_matters: Agent benchmarks help measure reliability and failure modes before broader autonomous deployment.

items.title: ParseBench: A Document Parsing Benchmark for AI Agents
items.why_it_matters: Semantic parsing evaluations matter when agents make decisions from structured documents.

items.title: Fail2Drive: Benchmarking Closed-Loop Driving Generalization
items.why_it_matters: Generalization benchmarks are critical for testing safety under distribution shift in autonomous systems.

items.title: Quantifying Explanation Consistency: The C-Score Metric for CAM-Based Explainability in Medical Image Classification
items.why_it_matters: Consistency metrics can improve confidence in explanations used in high-stakes medical settings.

items.title: sciwrite-lint: Verification Infrastructure for the Age of Science Vibe-Writing
items.why_it_matters: Verification tooling targets fabricated citations and integrity failures in AI-assisted scientific writing.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
